_README: This is the skeleton for the SECOND, separate lab.json copy: labs-platform/app/content/labs/<slug>/lab.json (NOT this labs-platform/labs/_template/ folder — copy this file's content there and delete this _README key, it isn't part of the real schema). See LAB-FACTORY.md sections 1 and 5 (H11) for why this file exists as a duplicate of ../<slug>/lab.json instead of being generated from it. Keep every overlapping field IDENTICAL to the engine-side lab.json in labs-platform/labs/<slug>/lab.json — this copy additionally feeds app/scripts/build-lab-content.mjs, which is what actually generates lib/lab-catalog.ts and lib/lab-content.ts (the files the app renders from). This copy does NOT include learnerPolicy — that field is only ever read from the engine-side copy.
title: TODO Title Case Lab Name
summary: TODO — must match the engine-side lab.json's summary verbatim (it's shown in two places: the app catalog card and, mirrored again, the marketing site's AWS_LABS entry).
learningObjectives: TODO — should match the engine-side copy.

successCriteria.description: TODO — this exact array (id/description) becomes labObjectives[slug] via build-lab-content.mjs, which is what the learner's objectives checklist (LabPanel) renders. The `verify` field, if present, is dropped by the generator — it's not needed here (it's documentation for the grader author, keep it in the engine-side copy).
successCriteria.verify: TODO — optional here; the generator only pulls id + description.

hints: TODO — should match the engine-side copy.
